steps.label: Objectives
steps.bodyHtml:
• 6.2.1 Combine Power Series by addition and subtraction
  • 6.2.2 Create a new power series by multiplication by a power of the variable or a constant, or by substitution
  • 6.2.3 Multiply two power series together
  • 6.2.4 Differentiate and Integrate power series term-by-term

steps.label: Theorem 6.2
steps.subtitle: Combining Power Series
steps.bodyHtml:
Suppose that the two power series \(\displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}c_nx^n\) and \(\displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}d_nx^n\) converge to the function f and g, respectively, on a common interval I.
  1. The power series \(\displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}(c_nx^n\pm d_nx^n)\) converges to \(f\pm g\) on I.
  2. For any integer \(m\geq0\) and any real number b, the power series \(\displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}bx^mc_nx^n\) converges to \(bx^mf(x)\) on I.
  3. For any integer \(m\geq 0\) and any real number b, the series \(\displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}c_n(bx^m)^n\) converges to \(f(bx^m)\) for all x such that \(bx^m\) is in I

steps.label: Constructing Power Series from Known Power Series
Theorem 6.4
steps.subtitle: Term-by-Term Differentiation and Integration for Power Series
steps.bodyHtml:
Suppose that the power series defined a function \(f(x)=\displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}c_n(x-a)^n\) and converges to the function on the interval \((a-R,a+R)\) for some R > 0. The derivative of the function f will be given by differentiation the series term-by-term
  \[f'(x)=\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}nc_n(x-a)^{n-1}\]\[=c_1+2c_2(x-a)+3c_3(x-a)^2+\cdots\]for x such that \(|x-a|\lt R\).
  
  Also, to find \(\displaystyle\int f(x)dx\), we can integrate the series term-by-term. The resulting series converges on \((a-R,a+R)\), and we have
  \[\int f(x)dx = \sum_{n=0}^{\infty}c_n\frac{(x-a)^{n+1}}{n+1}+C\]\[=C+c_0+c_1\frac{(x-a)^2}{2}+c_2\frac{(x-a)^3}{3}+\cdots\]for \(|x-a|\lt R\), where C is the constant of integration.
